mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in housing starts (HOUST) signal robust residential investment and broader economic momentum, leading to higher corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing starts lead GDP by 2-3 quarters
failure_condition: Rank IC of HOUST monthly change vs forward 60-day returns is negative over any rolling 3-year window, or the top tercile of HOUST growth periods underperforms the bottom tercile.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in the federal funds rate below 1.5% signals an accommodative monetary policy stance that reduces discount rates and encourages risk-taking, boosting equity prices over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Historically rate cuts below 1.5% correlate with positive equity returns
failure_condition: In sessions where FEDFUNDS <= 1.5, forward 20-day returns are not significantly positive (one-sided t-test p > 0.10) or actual returns in such periods are negative on average.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strength in both housing starts and retail sales indicates broad-based consumer and investment demand, providing a powerful signal that corporate revenues and earnings will improve, driving stock prices higher.
candidate_trigger: Both HOUST and RSAFS are above their respective 12-month moving averages
evidence_refs: fact:Coincident strength in housing and retail often precedes economic accelerations
failure_condition: When both triggers are active, forward 20-day returns are not on average higher than when only one or neither triggers are active, as measured by ANOVA or pairwise t-tests (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a tight labor market (UNRATE <= 4.5%), further increases in payroll employment exacerbate wage inflation fears and accelerate anticipated Fed tightening, which depresses equity valuations. Thus, positive PAYEMS surprises in this regime lead to negative S&P 500 returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Tight labor markets increase wage pressure and Fed tightening risk
failure_condition: When UNRATE <= 4.5, the correlation between PAYEMS surprises and forward 20-day returns is not negative and significant (Pearson r < 0, p < 0.05) over any 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Acceleration in consumer price inflation above 2.5% year-over-year erodes purchasing power and prompts anticipatory monetary tightening, which raises discount rates and compresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following month.
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation above 2.5% historically precedes Fed tightening episodes
failure_condition: In sessions where CPI YoY > 2.5%, forward 20-day returns are positive on average or the Sharpe ratio of those periods is above zero over any 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing output and supply-side capacity, which typically translates into higher corporate profits and employment, driving equity markets higher over a quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Industrial production is a coincident indicator of economic activity
failure_condition: Rank IC of INDPRO monthly change versus forward 60-day returns is negative or insignificant (p>0.10) over any rolling 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of low interest rates (FEDFUNDS) and strong GDP growth (GDPC1) creates a 'Goldilocks' environment for equities: easy monetary policy supports asset prices while robust demand drives earnings. When both conditions hold, stocks rally strongly over the next quarter.
evidence_refs: fact:Goldilocks economy historically coincides with bull markets
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns are not significantly positive (t-test p<0.05) when both conditions are met, or the strategy fails to outperform simple buy-and-hold over any 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate (rise of 0.2 percentage points or more in one month) signals a weakening labor market that leads to reduced consumer spending and corporate earnings, negatively impacting stock prices over the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Sudden jumps in unemployment are recessionary signals
failure_condition: In the 5 trading days following a UNRATE jump of >=0.2pp, the S&P 500 returns are not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05) or the hit rate of negative returns is less than 55%.
